title: Property API boundary + contract-version hardening (owner code-audit P0, prerequisite for Confirm)
objective: Backend/contracts half of the API boundary hardening, with contract 1.2.0 established as the current canonical property-profile contract (M2-T004 published it to the closed enum ['1.0.0','1.1.0','1.2.0']): enforce backend response validation of the property-profile payload against the selected canonical schema before send (validation runs against the correct contract version, never a silently hard-coded stale one); resolve and implement the contract_version DECLARATION decision that M2-T004 deferred here (README section 167 - the accepted builder still declares stale '1.0.0' while emitting keys through 1.2.0; decide which version the builder declares and whether declared-version-vs-emitted-key-set are validated against each other, with 1.2.0 as the canonical shape and 1.0.0/1.1.0 backward compatibility preserved because every added key is optional); add a deterministic TypeScript type-generation pipeline from the canonical 1.2.0 packages/contracts/schemas/v1/property_profile.schema.json replacing any handwritten competing representation; guarantee exact HTTP-status/state pairs at the API (mismatched pairs impossible to emit); emit an explicit bounded error for any unsupported/unknown contract version rather than a silent coercion; provide the recorded fixtures the frontend needs for the HTTP-500-plus-state-no_match regression
business_reason: Owner code-audit directive 2026-07-17 P0: boundary hardening must land before the Confirm screen consumes the API
inputs: Owner code-audit directive 2026-07-17 P0 Property-API items (verbatim in project-control/reports/OWNER-REVIEW-code-audit-task-consolidation.md section 2.2)

packages/contracts/schemas/v1/property_profile.schema.json AS SETTLED BY M2-T004 (contract 1.2.0 is now the current canonical contract - closed enum ['1.0.0','1.1.0','1.2.0']; dimensions, fact/observation identity, digest lineage already in place) + packages/contracts/README.md section 167 ('contract_version semantics - recorded as an INPUT to M2-T003': M2-T004 published 1.2.0 as the shape but deliberately did NOT decide which version a producer declares nor whether declared version and emitted key set are validated against each other; the accepted builder still declares '1.0.0'; M2-T003 owns that declaration/validation decision against the canonical 1.2.0 shape); the contract_version question is resolved once against this final shape (owner sequencing decision 2026-07-17: M2-T004 precedes this task so validation, typegen, fixtures, and the version declaration are implemented exactly once)
services/api/app/api/v1/properties.py + builder.py (current status/state emission: 422 validation_error, 404 state=no_match, 200 profile, 502/503/504 typed, 500 generic, X-Correlation-ID)
project-control/reports/M1-T005-G3-review.md section 5 + M1-T006-G3-review.md D5 (state as non-200 discriminator; partial district provenance)
apps/web/src/lib current handwritten API types (the competing representation this task replaces - read-only input; removal happens in M2-T002 when the client migrates)
outputs: Backend response validation: every 200 property-profile payload validated against the canonical schema before send; validation failure returns a typed 500 internal_contract_error with correlation id - an invalid 200 becomes impossible
Exact HTTP-status/state pair enforcement at the API: the documented pair matrix is the single source of truth; a test enumerates all emissions and rejects undocumented pairs

contract_version resolution against the canonical 1.2.0 shape: decision recorded in packages/contracts/README.md (superseding the section-167 deferral) and implemented consistently across schema, API, builder, and fixtures - the builder no longer declares the stale '1.0.0'; the declared version and the emitted key set agree; 1.0.0/1.1.0 remain valid and tested (every added key is optional) so previously accepted consumers/fixtures are unaffected

Backend runtime validation selects the correct canonical contract version (never a silently hard-coded stale version); an unsupported/unknown contract version yields an explicit bounded error (typed, correlation-id'd) rather than a silent coercion or an invalid 200

Deterministic TypeScript type-generation pipeline from the canonical 1.2.0 property_profile schema into packages/contracts/generated/ (committed output + CI drift check; no network, no new heavy toolchain on owner PC - generation runs in CI)
Recorded transport-level fixture for HTTP 500 + state=no_match (the frontend regression input for M2-T002)
acceptance_scenarios: S1 valid profile passes backend validation unchanged and declares the resolved canonical contract_version (1.2.0); all existing recorded-official fixtures still serve
S2 fault injection: a deliberately malformed profile (wrong type / missing required key) from the builder yields typed 500 internal_contract_error with correlation id - never an invalid 200
S3 pair matrix: parametrized test enumerates every (HTTP status, state) emission path against the documented matrix; an undocumented pair fails the suite
S4 the 500+state=no_match fixture is recorded and replayable through the existing fixture transport for M2-T002's client regression
S5 typegen determinism: regenerating types from the canonical 1.2.0 schema is byte-identical (CI drift check fails on divergence); generated types cover 100 percent of schema keys
S6 contract_version consistency: schema, README, API response, builder, and fixtures all agree on 1.2.0 as canonical; a mismatch test fails; no stale version is silently hard-coded anywhere

S7 backward compatibility: a valid 1.1.0 (and 1.0.0) profile instance still passes backend validation and is served unchanged - proving the additive-optional guarantee and that previously accepted consumers are not broken
S8 unsupported version: a profile declaring an unpublished contract_version (e.g. 1.3.0) is rejected with an explicit bounded error (typed, correlation-id'd), never coerced or silently accepted
S9 G1 re-verification: no official-source field mapping or semantics changed (diff of builder mapping reviewed by data-contract-verifier)
S10 regression: full API pytest suite + M2-T001 web-e2e job (recorded-official-fixture harness) stay green
risks: contract_version decision may ripple into stored fixtures - keep the change additive and re-record fixtures with provenance rather than hand-editing
Type-generation tool choice must run in CI without adding local toolchain weight (owner PC thin-client policy)
M2-T002 and M1-T009 depend on this task; it follows M2-T004 (owner-approved order M2-T004 -> M2-T003 -> M2-T002/M1-T009)
